subtopics.title: Types and Functions of Family
common_misconceptions: Nuclear family is the best type
Family only means parents and children
subtopics.teaching_note: Nigerian context: Extended family is common and culturally valued. Family provides: emotional support, economic support, socialization, protection, identity. Types: Nuclear (parents + children), Extended (grandparents, uncles, aunts), Polygamous (one man, multiple wives - common in Northern Nigeria and among Muslims), Monogamous (one husband, one wife). Family is the first agent of socialization - children learn language, values, customs.

subtopics.title: Nigerian Culture and Values
common_misconceptions: All Nigerians have the same culture
Modern culture is better than traditional
subtopics.teaching_note: Nigeria: over 250 ethnic groups. Major: Hausa-Fulani, Yoruba, Igbo. Each has unique language, dress, food, festivals. Material culture: tangible (clothing, food, houses, tools). Non-material: intangible (language, beliefs, values, customs). Nigerian values: respect for elders, hospitality (welcoming guests with food), communal living, hard work, religion. Cultural diversity: unity in diversity, Federal Character Principle. Challenges: ethnic conflicts, religious intolerance, cultural erosion from globalization.
